questions.q: The Kerala School is most famous for developing the foundations of which branch of mathematics centuries before Newton and Leibniz?
questions.explanation: The Kerala School developed infinite series and ideas of calculus, such as series for pi and trigonometric functions, long before Newton and Leibniz.

questions.q: Who is regarded as the founder of the Kerala School of mathematics and astronomy?
questions.explanation: Madhava of Sangamagrama, active around 1340-1425, is regarded as the founder of the Kerala School.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state did the Kerala School flourish?
questions.explanation: As its name indicates, the school flourished in the region of Kerala in southern India.

questions.q: The Kerala School is best known for finding infinite series for which famous mathematical constant?
questions.explanation: The school discovered infinite series that converge to pi, a key achievement attributed to Madhava.

questions.q: Roughly during which centuries was the Kerala School active?
questions.explanation: The Kerala School flourished from about the 14th to the 16th centuries CE.

questions.q: Besides mathematics, the Kerala School was also strongly devoted to which field?
questions.explanation: It is properly called the Kerala School of astronomy and mathematics, as its scholars studied both subjects deeply.

questions.q: The infinite series for the arctangent function found by the Kerala School is often named after which mathematician?
questions.explanation: The arctangent series is commonly called the Madhava-Gregory series, after Madhava who found it first.

questions.q: The Kerala School developed accurate infinite series expansions for which pair of trigonometric functions?
questions.explanation: Madhava and his successors derived the power series for sine and cosine, equivalent to the modern Taylor series.

questions.q: What was the primary written language of the Kerala School's mathematical and astronomical texts?
questions.explanation: The school's treatises were composed in Sanskrit, often in verse form following Indian scholarly tradition.

questions.q: The Kerala School's series for pi is essentially the same as which later European series?
questions.explanation: Madhava's series for pi is equivalent to the Leibniz series, discovered in Europe roughly three centuries later.

questions.q: Nilakantha Somayaji, a major Kerala School astronomer, wrote which influential astronomical treatise?
questions.explanation: Nilakantha Somayaji composed the Tantrasangraha around 1500, a key work of the Kerala School.

questions.q: The text Yuktibhasha, which gives detailed proofs of Kerala School results, was written in which language?
questions.explanation: Jyeshthadeva's Yuktibhasha was written in Malayalam, making it notable for presenting demonstrations in a regional language.

questions.q: Who is credited with composing the Yuktibhasha, regarded by some as an early text on calculus?
questions.explanation: Jyeshthadeva wrote the Yuktibhasha around 1530, giving the rationale and proofs behind earlier results.

questions.q: Nilakantha Somayaji proposed a partially heliocentric model in which the inner planets orbit what?
questions.explanation: In the Tantrasangraha, Nilakantha had Mercury and Venus orbit the Sun, while the Sun orbits the Earth, anticipating Tycho Brahe's model.

questions.q: Madhava is credited with one of the first uses of which mathematical idea, central to calculus?
options: The limit and infinite series summation
questions.explanation: Madhava worked with infinite series and limiting processes, ideas that lie at the heart of calculus.

questions.q: Madhava's series for pi included correction terms that greatly improved its convergence. What did these terms achieve?
options: They gave faster, more accurate approximations of pi
questions.explanation: Madhava added end-correction terms to the slowly converging series, yielding remarkably accurate values of pi.

questions.q: Parameshvara, a member of the Kerala School lineage, is especially noted for developing what kind of astronomical model?
options: The drgganita system based on observation
questions.explanation: Parameshvara developed the Drgganita system, revising astronomical parameters to better match observations.

questions.q: Madhava is sometimes regarded by historians as a forerunner of which mathematical concept named for a later mathematician?
questions.explanation: His power series for sine and cosine are essentially the Taylor (Maclaurin) series, derived centuries before Brook Taylor.

questions.q: Which Kerala School text by Sankara Variyar serves as a commentary explaining the Tantrasangraha?
questions.explanation: Sankara Variyar wrote the Yuktidipika, a commentary on Nilakantha's Tantrasangraha.

questions.q: The Kerala School represents a continuation of the mathematical tradition of which earlier Indian astronomer-mathematician?
questions.explanation: The school built on the work of Aryabhata, and Nilakantha even wrote an important commentary on the Aryabhatiya.

questions.q: How many decimal places of pi could be obtained from Madhava's most accurate value?
questions.explanation: Madhava's best value of pi is accurate to about 11 decimal places, an extraordinary achievement for his era.

questions.q: Most of Madhava's original mathematical works are lost, and his results are known mainly through what?
options: Writings of his successors and commentators
questions.explanation: Madhava's discoveries survive chiefly through citations by later Kerala School scholars such as Nilakantha and Jyeshthadeva.

questions.q: The Madhava-Newton style power series for the sine function is expressed in terms of powers of which quantity?
questions.explanation: Madhava expressed the sine as an infinite series in powers of the arc, equivalent to the modern sine power series.

questions.q: Parameshvara, an early figure linked to the school, is recorded as having made systematic observations over roughly how long a period?
questions.explanation: Parameshvara is noted for carrying out astronomical observations over an unusually long span, reported as a period of about fifty-five years.

questions.q: Which lineage best reflects the teacher-student succession within the Kerala School?
questions.explanation: The transmission ran roughly from Madhava to Parameshvara to Damodara to Nilakantha, preserving the school's results.

questions.q: The Kerala School's results on summing series effectively used what mathematical technique foundational to integral calculus?
questions.explanation: They summed series of powers and passed to limiting sums, a procedure equivalent to early integration.

questions.q: The hypothesis that Kerala School ideas may have reached Europe often points to contact through whom?
questions.explanation: Some historians have proposed, though it remains unproven, that Jesuit missionaries could have transmitted Kerala results to Europe.

questions.q: Madhava's mnemonic verse for pi gives a value associated with which approximate figure?
questions.explanation: Madhava's encoded value of pi corresponds to approximately 3.14159265359, correct to about eleven digits.

questions.q: Nilakantha's correction to the lunar and planetary equations of center reflected his use of what?
questions.explanation: Nilakantha refined planetary models using better geometry and observational corrections in the Tantrasangraha.

questions.q: Why is the Kerala School historically significant in the global history of mathematics?
options: It anticipated key results of calculus and infinite series before Europe
questions.explanation: The school anticipated infinite series, power series for trigonometric functions, and ideas of calculus centuries before their European discovery.
